title: EoE Continuity Error
wikitext:
[]: EoE Continuity Error?             Quote Post by Lurkis » Tue Oct 09, 2012 7:34 pm I just finished watching EoE again and I noticed something after Asuka defeats the MP Evas. I understand they weren't destroyed due to their intact S2 engines and it is also shown that the wounds they suffer were not regenerated during the reactivation sequence. With that being said, I was wondering what happened to the MP Eva who was sliced in two. Did this Eva somehow reconnect with its lower half? Or was it flying around without legs? At first I thought maybe only 8 had reactivated, but when the tree of life is formed around unit 01, it shows all 9 MP Eva's taking a position.

[]:             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Tue Oct 09, 2012 8:43 pm That scene has, as I recall, officially-acknowledged 'animation errors.' Yeah, to my memory, the half a torso was supposed to be regarded as flying about.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Oct 09, 2012 9:42 pm Animation errors = Epic Eva Battle I really couldn't complain.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Tue Oct 09, 2012 9:59 pm It's Evangelions it ain't gotta explain shit http://wiki.evageeks.org/Guides:Specific_Mass_Production_Evangelions

[]:             Quote Post by Kamon-san » Tue Oct 09, 2012 10:42 pm Speaking of continuity errors, I noticed something odd I couldn't believe I didn't notice before the last time I watched EoE. When the UN troops destroy EVA 02's umbilical cable, she is supposed to only have a few minutes of energy left, however she goes on to destroy a couple of fighter planes, looks at the MP Evas as they slowly descend into the Geofront, receives an order from Misato to destroy them, and THEN we get this long scene of Misato's last words to Shinji. Just how long is EVA 02's operational time without the umbilical cable in this movie? XD This reminds me of Frieza's estimation that planet Namek would ''explode in 5 minutes''

[]:             Quote Post by Lurkis » Wed Oct 10, 2012 2:05 am Fireball wrote: It's Evangelions it ain't gotta explain shit http://wiki.evageeks.org/Guides:Specific_Mass_Production_Evangelions

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Wed Oct 10, 2012 2:05 am Kamon-san wrote: Speaking of continuity errors, I noticed something odd I couldn't believe I didn't notice before the last time I watched EoE. When the UN troops destroy EVA 02's umbilical cable, she is supposed to only have a few minutes of energy left, however she goes on to destroy a couple of fighter planes, looks at the MP Evas as they slowly descend into the Geofront, receives an order from Misato to destroy them, and THEN we get this long scene of Misato's last words to Shinji. Just how long is EVA 02's operational time without the umbilical cable in this movie? XD This reminds me of Frieza's estimation that planet Namek would ''explode in 5 minutes''

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Wed Oct 10, 2012 5:40 am Kamon-san wrote: When the UN troops destroy EVA 02's umbilical cable, she is supposed to only have a few minutes of energy left, however she goes on to destroy a couple of fighter planes, looks at the MP Evas as they slowly descend into the Geofront, receives an order from Misato to destroy them, and THEN we get this long scene of Misato's last words to Shinji. Just how long is EVA 02's operational time without the umbilical cable in this movie? XD This reminds me of Frieza's estimation that planet Namek would ''explode in 5 minutes''

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Wed Oct 10, 2012 9:10 am It's hard to tell how much time does pass, exactly. For one thing, a lot of it seems to operate on relative time -- we view the battle from Asuka's POV, with time stretched and in slow motion. The Ritsuko cutaways make things even harder to pin down.

[]:             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Wed Oct 10, 2012 1:07 pm Who knows how 02 being berserk interacted with its non-plugged lifespan, either? We're led to believe that Asuka's seconds are numbered the moment the cord is severed, but it isn't as if this is the first time an Evangelion has continued combat without it being possible.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Oct 10, 2012 3:59 pm It's a bit of a moot point, since even deploying the MPEs should have taken a lot longer than three and a half minutes. Dramatic license!

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Wed Oct 10, 2012 4:22 pm Gainax is riddled with production continuity errors throughout Evangelion. It's worse in their earlier series. Multiple producers associated with the series noted that the production environment was at best "chaotic" (like almost every GAINAX project, even now), and that they were bouncing animation between several different production houses. Eventually, things like that are going to slip through the cracks

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Oct 10, 2012 4:34 pm ran1 wrote: Gainax is riddled with production continuity errors throughout Evangelion. It's worse in their earlier series. Multiple producers associated with the series noted that the production environment was at best "chaotic" (like almost every GAINAX project, even now), and that they were bouncing animation between several different production houses. Eventually, things like that are going to slip through the cracks

[]:             Quote Post by Lurkis » Wed Oct 10, 2012 5:48 pm Bagheera wrote: It's a bit of a moot point, since even deploying the MPEs should have taken a lot longer than three and a half minutes. Dramatic license!

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Oct 10, 2012 6:01 pm Lurkis wrote: They were probably already flying in the area as a 'just in case' scenario. Seems like something SEELE would do.

[]:             Quote Post by Lurkis » Wed Oct 10, 2012 6:09 pm Bagheera wrote: No, I mean just dropping them off and getting them to the ground. That takes time!

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Oct 10, 2012 6:33 pm Lurkis wrote: I still don't think it would take an unrealistic amount of time. Skydiving usually takes about 40 seconds (friend went out the other month) so let's bump that up to a minute considering the MP spread their wings and circled for a moment. That leaves Asuka 40 seconds to finish off the JSSDF forces between her chord being severed and the MP landing.

[]:             Quote Post by Lurkis » Wed Oct 10, 2012 6:45 pm Bagheera wrote: No, the real question here is why no one along the line said "wait, her cable's been cut. Why are we doing this again?" If they know enough to know the cable's important this is the first question that should have come to mind. And yet . . .

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Wed Oct 10, 2012 7:49 pm Lurkis wrote: Yea I gotta agree with you on that, why not just wait her out? o.O

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Oct 10, 2012 7:55 pm Stryker wrote: Because nobody benefits from protracted conflict. Even if Asuka's doom was inevitable, she still will have inflicted heavy damage, and casualties.
summary: []: EoE Continuity Error?             Quote Post by Lurkis » Tue Oct 09, 2012 7:34 pm I just finished watching EoE again and I noticed something after Asuka defeats the MP Evas. I understand they weren't destroyed due to their intact S2 engines and it is also shown that the wounds they suffer were not regenerated during the reactivation sequence. With that being said, I was wondering what happened to the MP Eva who was sliced in two. Di
